indication: 1 previous line of patients which contained lenalidomide and their condition is either refractory to lenalidomide or they cannot tolerate lenalidomide

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1-8 given with bortezomib. From cycle 9 onwards belantamab monotherapy continues. Premeds mandatory on C1D1. Patient to drink 3 litres water per day.
Subcutaneous injection. Cycles 1-8 only. VZV prophylaxis mandatory.

Reduce to 10mg in patients over 75 years old. Take in the morning with or after food. Day 1 dose acts as premed — administer 60-180 minutes prior to belantamab infusion. Can be given as 20mg IV bolus if patient has not taken oral dose.

pre_medications.timing: 60 minutes prior to belantamab mafodotin infusion on C1D1
60 minutes prior to belantamab mafodotin infusion
pre_medications.indication: Infusion-related reaction prophylaxis / included in regimen dose

take_home_medicines.frequency: Days 2,8,9,15,16,22,23 of each cycle
take_home_medicines.notes: Reduce to 10mg in patients over 75 years. Dispense all days on day 1 of cycle.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 28 days (VZV prophylaxis)
take_home_medicines.notes: Anti-infective prophylaxis — VZV prophylaxis mandatory for bortezomib

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day on Monday, Wednesday and Friday only for 28 days

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day for 7 days — cycle one only
take_home_medicines.notes: TLS prophylaxis. First cycle only.

take_home_medicines.frequency: 1 drop both eyes four times a day from first day of infusion until completion of treatment
take_home_medicines.notes: Ocular protection. Supply 21 days.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once daily
take_home_medicines.notes: As per local formulary choice. For patients at high risk of GI ulceration.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Three times a day when required

dose_modifications.notes: ANC must be ≥1.0x10^9/L to initiate new cycle of belantamab. Consider withholding and/or reducing dose to 1.9mg/kg. Consider G-CSF support. Seek consultant advice.
Consider erythropoietin stimulating agents. Haemoglobin must be ≥80g/L to initiate new belantamab cycle.
Platelets must be ≥50x10^9/L to initiate new belantamab cycle.

Consider withholding belantamab and/or reducing dose to 1.9mg/kg. [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]

Withhold belantamab until platelet count improves to Grade 3 or better. Consider resuming at reduced dose of 1.9mg/kg. [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]
Consider treatment delay, dose reduction, or platelet transfusion. Seek consultant advice.
Consider treatment delay, dose reduction, or G-CSF support. Seek consultant advice.

Interrupt infusion and provide supportive treatment. Once symptoms resolve, resume at lower infusion rate reduced by at least 50%. [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]

Interrupt infusion and provide supportive treatment. If anaphylactic or life-threatening, permanently discontinue. Otherwise resume at lower infusion rate reduced by at least 50%. [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]
Initiate at 0.7mg/m². May escalate to 1mg/m² or reduce to 0.5mg/m² in subsequent cycles based on tolerability.
Clinical decision required.
Hold belantamab dose until ACR drops to 50 or below.

dose_modifications.condition: Mild superficial keratopathy / BCVA decline 1 line on Snellen
dose_modifications.notes: Continue treatment at current dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Moderate/severe keratopathy or BCVA decline ≥2 lines (Snellen not worse than 20/200)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until improvement to Grade 1 or better. Resume at reduced dose level 1 (1.9mg/kg). [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]

dose_modifications.condition: Corneal epithelial defect or BCVA decline to Snellen >20/200
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until Grade 1 or better. Resume at 1.9mg/kg if applicable. For worsening symptoms unresponsive to dose reductions, consider permanent discontinuation. [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]
Continue treatment when toxicity resolves to Grade 0-1.
Permanently discontinue. [VERIFIED: Belantamab mafodotin dose reduction to 1.9mg/kg is permitted per MHRA SmPC for ocular toxicity — this is antibody-drug conjugate specific]

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 1 without pain or loss of function
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with full dose bortezomib.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 1 with pain or Grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce bortezomib dose to 1mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 2 with pain or Grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue until resolved to Grade 1 or less, then reinitiate at 0.7mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 4 or severe autonomic neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue bortezomib permanently.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle of belantamab mafodotin: ANC ≥1.0x10^9/L, Haemoglobin ≥80g/L, Platelets ≥50x10^9/L. Non-haematological toxicity should resolve to NCI-CTC grade 1 or baseline.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of treatment
Calcium and magnesium levels at regular intervals throughout treatment
Immunoglobulins and paraprotein monthly
VTE risk assessment prior to starting treatment
Ophthalmology assessments every cycle before belantamab dosing for first 4 doses and based on clinic need after cycle 4
Regular monitoring of blood glucose due to dexamethasone use
special_warnings: Belantamab mafodotin: ocular toxicity (keratitis, blurred vision) — ophthalmology review mandatory every cycle for first 4 doses
Bortezomib: subcutaneous route preferred to reduce peripheral neuropathy risk
VZV prophylaxis is mandatory with bortezomib
Preserve-free artificial tears QDS from first day of infusion throughout treatment
This protocol may require funding approval
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy
Adequate hydration essential — potential for tumour lysis syndrome
Thromboprophylaxis required for patients with additional VTE risk factors

required_patient_fields.weight_kg: Required for belantamab mafodotin dose calculation (mg/kg)
required_patient_fields.vzv_prophylaxis_confirmed: VZV prophylaxis (aciclovir 400mg BD) mandatory before bortezomib
required_patient_fields.ophthalmology_review: Ophthalmology assessment required before each belantamab dose for first 4 cycles
required_patient_fields.vte_risk_assessment: VTE risk assessment mandatory prior to treatment

drugs.special_instructions: Reduce to 10mg in patients over 75 years old. Take in the morning with or after food. Day 1 dose acts as premed — administer 60-180 minutes prior to belantamab infusion. Can be given as 20mg IV bolus if patient has not taken oral dose.
drugs.notes: Days 1,2,8,9,15,16,22,23 of each cycle | Reduce to 10mg in patients over 75 years old. Take in the morning with or after food. Day 1 dose acts as premed — administer 60-180 minutes prior to belantamab infusion. Can be given as 20mg IV bolus if patient has not taken oral dose.

indication: Carfilzomib in combination with dexamethasone is an option for treating multiple myeloma in adults if the patient has had one previous therapy which did not include bortezomib. Treatment intent – disease modification. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycle 1 days 1-2 only: 20mg/m² (max 44mg). Dose escalation to 56mg/m² from day 8 cycle 1 onwards if tolerated. Dose capped at 2.2m². Dose banded per nationally agreed 2mg/ml bands.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Days 1,2,8,9,15,16 of cycles 2-12; days 8,9,15,16 of cycle 1
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Normal dose 56mg/m² (max 123mg). Dose level reductions: 1st 45mg/m², 2nd 36mg/m², 3rd 27mg/m². Dexamethasone must be administered prior to carfilzomib.

Administer at least 30 minutes and up to 4 hours prior to carfilzomib infusion on carfilzomib days. Take in the morning with or after food. Dose level reductions: starting 20mg, level 1 10mg. If toxicity recovery prolonged beyond 14 days, decrease by one dose level.

pre_medications.timing: At least 30 minutes and up to 4 hours prior to carfilzomib infusion
pre_medications.indication: Reduces incidence and severity of infusion-related reactions — part of regimen dose

Post-hydration after carfilzomib infusion over 30 minutes

take_home_medicines.notes: Take with or after food, once a day in the morning.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day on Monday, Wednesday and Friday for 28 days

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: Anti-infective prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.notes: For patients at high risk of GI ulceration.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day
take_home_medicines.notes: Anti-fungal prophylaxis.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold dose until neutrophils recover to ≥0.5x10^9/L. 1st occurrence: restart at current dose level. 2nd occurrence: restart and consider 1 dose level reduction.
Withhold dose until platelets ≥10x10^9/L. 1st occurrence: continue at current dose. 2nd occurrence: consider 1 dose level reduction.

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine ≥2x baseline, CrCl <15ml/min, or CrCl decreased ≤50% baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold dose. Restart carfilzomib when renal function recovered to within 25% of baseline (consider 1 dose level reduction).
Stop until toxicity resolved/returned to baseline. Consider restarting at 1 dose level reduction. Dose levels: 56mg/m², 45mg/m², 36mg/m², 27mg/m².
Maintain dose and treat with H2 antagonist or PPI. Decrease by one dose level if symptoms persist.
Interrupt dose until symptoms controlled. Add H2 blocker or PPI. Decrease one dose level when restarted.
Use diuretics as needed and decrease dose by one level.
Decrease dose by one dose level. Treat with insulin or oral hypoglycaemic agents as needed.
Discontinue dexamethasone from treatment regimen.

dose_modifications.condition: Recovery from toxicities prolonged beyond 14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Decrease dexamethasone dose by one dose level.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to starting new cycle: neutrophils ≥1x10^9/L, platelets ≥50x10^9/L, non-haematological toxicity should resolve to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below or baseline.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFT and U&Es prior to day 1 of treatment
Regular monitoring of blood glucose — good practice due to dexamethasone
special_warnings: Carfilzomib infusion-related reactions can occur immediately or up to 24 hours after administration — dexamethasone must be given prior to reduce incidence
Pre- and post-hydration with sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml over 30 minutes required
If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped
Carfilzomib is neutral for extravasation
Dose banded in accordance with nationally agreed dose bands (2mg/ml); dose capped at 2.2m²
12 cycles will be set in ARIA system

drugs.special_instructions: Cycle 1 days 1-2 only: 20mg/m² (max 44mg). Dose escalation to 56mg/m² from day 8 cycle 1 onwards if tolerated. Dose capped at 2.2m². Dose banded per nationally agreed 2mg/ml bands.
drugs.notes: Cycle 1 days 1 and 2 only (escalation dose) | Cycle 1 days 1-2 only: 20mg/m² (max 44mg). Dose escalation to 56mg/m² from day 8 cycle 1 onwards if tolerated. Dose capped at 2.2m². drugs.special_instructions: Administer at least 30 minutes and up to 4 hours prior to carfilzomib infusion on carfilzomib days. Take in the morning with or after food. Dose level reductions: starting 20mg, level 1 10mg. If toxicity recovery prolonged beyond 14 days, decrease by one dose level.

indication: Carfilzomib in combination with lenalidomide and dexamethasone is an option for treating multiple myeloma in adults if the patient has had one previous therapy which included bortezomib. Treatment intent – disease modification. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycle 1 days 1-2 only: 20mg/m² (max 44mg) over 10 minutes. Escalate to 27mg/m² from day 8 cycle 1 onwards. Dose capped at 2.2m². Carfilzomib stops after 18 cycles.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until ≥0.5x10^9/L. 1st: restart at current dose. 2nd: consider 1 dose level reduction. Levels: 27mg/m², 20mg/m², 15mg/m².
Withhold until ≥10x10^9/L. 1st: continue at current dose. 2nd: consider 1 dose level reduction.
1st: consider G-CSF, delay until recovery, restart at full dose. 2nd: restart at 15mg. 3rd: restart at 10mg. Subsequent: restart at 5mg.
1st: delay until recovery, restart at 15mg. 2nd: restart at 10mg. 3rd: restart at 5mg. Dose levels: 25mg, 15mg, 10mg, 5mg.
Start at 10mg once a day. Can increase to 15mg after 2 cycles if no response.
Start at 15mg on alternate days.
5mg once a day. On dialysis days administer following dialysis.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold. Restart when recovered to within 25% of baseline (consider 1 dose level reduction).

dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment and start full anticoagulation. May be reinstated at clinician's discretion once fully anticoagulated.

Discontinue dexamethasone from treatment regimen.

required_patient_fields.prior_bortezomib_details: If prior bortezomib: must confirm ≥6-month proteasome inhibitor treatment-free interval from last bortezomib dose
required_patient_fields.prior_lines_of_therapy: Must have received exactly 1 prior line of therapy

drugs.special_instructions: 56mg/m² (max 120mg, equates to 2.2m² dose capped). 42-day cycle. Dose level reductions: 56mg/m², 45mg/m², 36mg/m², 27mg/m². Dexamethasone must be given prior. Pre- and post-hydration required.
drugs.notes: Days 1, 15, 29 of each 42-day cycle | 56mg/m² (max 120mg, equates to 2.2m² dose capped). 42-day cycle. Dose level reductions: 56mg/m², 45mg/m², 36mg/m², 27mg/m². Dexamethasone must be given prior. Pre- and post-hydration required.

drugs.special_instructions: Administer at least 30 minutes and up to 4 hours prior to carfilzomib on carfilzomib days. Take in morning with or after food. Dose level reductions: starting 20mg, level 1 10mg.
drugs.notes: Days 1,2,15,16,29,30 of each 42-day cycle | Administer at least 30 minutes and up to 4 hours prior to carfilzomib on carfilzomib days. Take in morning with or after food. Dose level reductions: starting 20mg, level 1 10mg.

indication: Pomalidomide, in combination with low-dose dexamethasone, is recommended as an option for treating multiple myeloma in adults at third or subsequent relapse; that is, after 3 previous treatments including both lenalidomide and bortezomib. This protocol with a dose of dexamethasone of 20mg is recommended for those aged over 75 years.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Once a day days 1-21 of each 28-day cycle
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Oral chemotherapy. Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory. Take at same time each day. Swallow whole. May cause drowsiness — take at night. Available as 1mg, 2mg, 3mg, 4mg capsules. All prescriptions accompanied by ePAF.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Twice a day days 1-28 of each cycle
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Check for drug interactions. Available as 250mg and 500mg tablets.
20mg weekly dose for patients aged over 75 years. Take in morning with or immediately after food. Dose level reductions: starting 20mg, level 2 12mg, level 1 8mg.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: Continuous throughout cycle.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Days 1, 8, 15 and 22 of each cycle
take_home_medicines.notes: Take in morning with or after food.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once daily days 1-21
take_home_medicines.notes: Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory. Oral chemotherapy alert applies.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: Anti-infective prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day on Monday, Wednesday and Friday only for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: PCP prophylaxis. May dispense as 480mg twice a day on Mon/Wed/Fri.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: Anti-fungal prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day for 7 days — first cycle only
take_home_medicines.notes: TLS prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: As per risk assessment
take_home_medicines.notes: Aspirin 75mg daily for low risk. LMWH (dalteparin 5000 units OD, enoxaparin 40mg OD, or heparin 5000 units BD) for high risk. Supply 28 days.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once daily as per local formulary
take_home_medicines.notes: For patients at high risk of GI ulceration.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils < 0.5x10^9/L or febrile neutropenia (fever ≥38.5°C and neutrophils <1)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt pomalidomide and monitor FBC weekly. Once neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L resume at 3mg. For each subsequent drop below 0.5x10^9/L: resume at 1mg less than previous dose.
Interrupt pomalidomide and monitor FBC weekly. Once platelets recover to ≥50x10^9/L resume at 3mg. For each subsequent drop: resume at 1mg less than previous dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Venous thrombosis or embolism NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment. Start full anticoagulation. May be reinstated at clinician's discretion once fully anticoagulated.
Interrupt or discontinue pomalidomide. Must discontinue for angioedema, grade 4 rash, SJS, TEN, DRESS.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 adverse reactions related to pomalidomide
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment. Restart when resolved to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below at 1mg less than previous dose.
Maintain dose and treat with H2 antagonist or PPI. Decrease by one dose level if symptoms persist.
Discontinue dexamethasone from treatment regimen.

dose_modifications.condition: Recovery from toxicities prolonged beyond 14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Decrease dexamethasone dose by one dose level.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate new cycle: neutrophil count ≥1x10^9/L and platelet count ≥50x10^9/L. No dose reductions necessary for clarithromycin or dexamethasone for haematological toxicity — modifications apply to pomalidomide only.

monitoring_requirements: FBC at baseline, then every 2 weeks for first 8 weeks of treatment, thereafter prior to each cycle
U&Es, Ca2+ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
Negative pregnancy test within 3 days prior to starting pomalidomide — repeat every 4 weeks (every 2 weeks in women with irregular cycles), final test 4 weeks after last dose
HBV testing before initiating pomalidomide
VTE risk assessment prior to treatment
Regular monitoring of blood glucose — good practice
LFT monitoring for first 6 months — pomalidomide associated with hepatotoxicity
Cardiac monitoring — pomalidomide associated with cardiac failure and atrial fibrillation
special_warnings: Pomalidomide: Pregnancy Prevention Programme (PPP) mandatory — structurally related to thalidomide, teratogenic
Pomalidomide: must not be taken during pregnancy; contraception required for at least 4 weeks before, during, and 4 weeks after treatment
DVT/PE prophylaxis mandatory with pomalidomide
Clarithromycin and pomalidomide interact with many other medications — always check drug interactions
Pomalidomide: cardiac failure and atrial fibrillation risk — monitor cardiac symptoms
Pomalidomide: interstitial lung disease risk — assess acute pulmonary symptoms
Pomalidomide: HBV reactivation — all patients screened before treatment
All prescriptions for pomalidomide accompanied by ePAF
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy
Pomalidomide only prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist
If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped
Dexamethasone starting dose levels: 20mg (starting), 12mg (level 2), 8mg (level 1)

indication: Daratumumab monotherapy is recommended as an option for treating relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma in adults whose previous therapy included a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulator ('imid') as a fourth line of treatment, that is, after 3 previous treatments.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Cycles 1-2: weekly (days 1,8,15,22); Cycles 3-6: fortnightly (days 1,15); Cycle 7 onwards: monthly (day 1 only)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Fixed dose 1800mg. Subcutaneous injection into abdomen approximately 7.5cm to right or left of umbilicus over approximately 3-5 minutes. Rotate injection sites. Do NOT inject into areas where skin is red, bruised, tender, hard or scarred. For patients >120kg consider IV preparation. Observe for 4 hours after first dose. If no major IRR after first dose, subsequent doses do not require 4-hour observation unless IRR occurs.

pre_medications.timing: 1-3 hours prior to daratumumab injection every dose
pre_medications.indication: IRR prophylaxis. Options: chlorphenamine 4mg, loratadine 10mg, cetirizine 10mg, fexofenadine 120mg, or acrivastine 8mg oral.

pre_medications.timing: 1-3 hours prior to daratumumab injection. Cycles 1-2: 20mg oral. Can be reduced to 10mg oral from cycle 3 onwards.
1-3 hours prior to daratumumab injection every dose
Prior to injection for first cycle only

take_home_medicines.frequency: Each morning for 2 days starting the day after each injection (post-injection corticosteroid)
take_home_medicines.notes: To reduce risk of delayed IRRs. Take in morning with or after food.

take_home_medicines.frequency: On the days of daratumumab administration — 1-3 hours prior
take_home_medicines.notes: As per local formulary choice. Supply 1 x OP to cover all cycles.

take_home_medicines.frequency: On the days of daratumumab administration — 1-3 hours prior
take_home_medicines.notes: Supply 1 x 100 x 500mg to cover all cycles.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: Anti-infective prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day on Monday, Wednesday and Friday only for 28 days
take_home_medicines.notes: PCP prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once a day for 7 days — first cycle only
take_home_medicines.notes: TLS prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Once daily as per local formulary
take_home_medicines.notes: For patients at high risk of GI ulceration.

dose_modifications.notes: No dose reductions recommended. Dose delay only. Neutrophils must be ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥50x10^9/L to initiate new cycle (unless counts are due to bone marrow infiltration — may administer at consultant discretion with blood products and G-CSF support).

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils < 0.5x10^9/L or febrile neutropenia (fever ≥38.5°C and neutrophils <1)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L resume daratumumab.
Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once platelets recover to ≥50x10^9/L resume daratumumab.
Discontinue daratumumab immediately and permanently. Institute appropriate emergency care.
Daratumumab must be stopped if HBV reactivation occurs.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate new cycle: neutrophils ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥50x10^9/L, unless low counts due to bone marrow infiltration with myeloma — may administer at consultant discretion. No dose reductions — delays only. Post-injection observation 4 hours first dose.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, Ca2+ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
HBV, hepatitis C, CMV and HIV testing before initiating daratumumab
Blood typing and screening prior to daratumumab — red cell phenotyping required as daratumumab binds CD38 on RBCs causing positive indirect antiglobulin test (Coombs)
All patients issued an identification card carried for 6 months after stopping therapy
Monitor for clinical and laboratory signs of HBV reactivation during treatment and for at least 6 months after completion
Regular monitoring of blood glucose due to dexamethasone use
Monitor for IRR signs and symptoms during and following injection — particularly first and second injections
All instances of infusion/injection related reactions recorded on ARIA
special_warnings: Daratumumab binds CD38 on red blood cells — interferes with compatibility testing including antibody screening and cross-matching (positive indirect antiglobulin test / Coombs test may persist up to 6 months after last dose). Blood transfusion laboratory must be informed. Red cell phenotyping required.
Daratumumab detectable on SPE and IFE assays — may cause false positive results for IgG kappa myeloma protein impacting complete response assessment
HBV reactivation reported — all patients screened before treatment; monitoring required during and for at least 6 months after treatment
Daratumumab SC can cause severe/serious IRRs including anaphylaxis — 11% incidence in clinical studies; most with first injection
Premedication with antihistamine, antipyretic, and corticosteroid mandatory prior to every injection
Post-injection corticosteroid (dexamethasone 4mg) required for 2 days after each injection to reduce delayed IRR risk
For patients >120kg consider IV preparation
First dose: cannula should be inserted for emergency anaphylaxis treatment; observe for 4 hours
Patients with history of COPD: post-injection bronchodilators and inhaled corticosteroids should be considered
Do not inject into areas where skin is red, bruised, tender, hard or scarred
Injection sites should be rotated
Dosing schedule: cycles 1-2 weekly (days 1,8,15,22), cycles 3-6 fortnightly (days 1,15), cycle 7 onwards monthly (day 1)

required_patient_fields.weight_kg: Required — if >120kg consider IV daratumumab preparation instead
required_patient_fields.hbv_hcv_cmv_hiv_status: HBV, hepatitis C, CMV and HIV screening mandatory before treatment
required_patient_fields.blood_group_and_screen: Blood typing and screening required prior to treatment; red cell phenotyping needed
required_patient_fields.blood_transfusion_lab_informed: Blood transfusion laboratory must be informed patient is receiving daratumumab
required_patient_fields.patient_id_card_issued: Identification card must be issued and carried for 6 months after stopping therapy
required_patient_fields.prior_treatments: Must have received at least 3 prior treatments including a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulator

Do NOT inject into areas where skin is red, bruised, tender, hard or scarred. For patients >120kg consider IV preparation. Observe for 4 hours after first dose. If no major IRR after first dose, subsequent doses do not require 4-hour observation unless IRR occurs.
